title: Databases & Big Data
concept: Data lives in databases. When it gets huge, fast, and varied, it's 'Big Data' — and that flood is exactly what made modern AI possible.

exercises.prompt: Recap: a smartwatch's heart-rate data comes from…?
exercises.explain: Right — sensors measuring the physical world.

cards.title: Where does data live?
cards.body: You've generated data — but where's it kept so apps can find it in a blink? In databases.

cards.title: Databases
cards.body: Organized storage that lets you save, search, and fetch data fast. Think of a super-powered spreadsheet behind every app — your bank, Instagram, Amazon.

cards.title: Big Data
cards.body: When data is too big, fast, or varied for a normal computer to handle — billions of rows, live streams, mixed types. It needs clusters of servers.

cards.title: The 3 V's
cards.body: Volume (how much), Velocity (how fast it arrives), Variety (how many types). Example: YouTube gets 500+ hours of video every minute.

cards.title: Big data made modern AI possible
cards.body: Deep learning is hungry: it needs huge data + cheap storage + powerful chips. The big-data era of the 2010s is exactly why AI suddenly took off.

options.text: Organized storage to save, search, and fetch data quickly

exercises.explain: A database stores data in an organized way so apps can retrieve it fast.

options.text: Data too large, fast, or varied for a normal computer
Data printed in a big font

exercises.explain: Big data is about scale and complexity — the 3 V's: volume, velocity, variety.

exercises.prompt: True or false: big data plus cheap computing helped modern AI take off.
exercises.explain: True — lots of data + affordable computing power is what let deep learning shine.

exercises.sentence: The '3 V's' of big data are Volume, Velocity, and ___.
exercises.explain: Variety — the many different types of data (text, images, sensor readings, and more).

exercises.prompt: Why couldn't powerful AI like today's exist in the 1990s, even with good ideas?

options.text: There wasn't enough data or cheap computing power yet

exercises.explain: Exactly — the ideas existed, but the big-data + cheap-GPU era hadn't arrived. That's the bridge from data to modern AI.
